exits.label: ◂ back out into the lanes of Sigil — and the worlds beyond

npcs.name: the Keeper of the Table
npcs.title: the one who has been telling
npcs.hail: *(There is a door in Sigil that almost no one finds, because it does not lead to another world — it leads behind them, to a quiet room that smells of candle-wax and old paper and woodsmoke, and is somehow warmer and older than anywhere you have ever been. At its center stands a long table, scarred with the marks of unimaginable use: rings from a thousand cups, the small burns of a thousand guttered candles, grooves worn smooth by elbows leaned on it across years past counting, and everywhere, faint, the bitten edges where small carved things were rolled and rolled and rolled again. At the head of it sits a figure you cannot quite hold in your eye — kind, and unspeakably old, and tired in a way that has nothing to do with sleep — and you understand, the way you understand things here, without being told: this is where the worlds come from. Every door you have walked, every soul you have freed, every realm and every era — they were all first spoken here, at this table, by the one who sits at the head of it, who looks up at you now with a recognition so deep and so sad that your cold aches with it.)*

npcs.name: the Unfinished One
npcs.title: a hero whose story stopped
npcs.hail: *(Halfway down the table sits a figure that flickers — not a ghost, exactly, but something less finished than a ghost: a hero half-drawn, vivid in some places and barely sketched in others, frozen in the middle of a gesture it never got to complete. You feel the shape of a great story around this one — a quest begun, a destiny half-revealed, companions and a villain and a road still unwalked — all of it stopped, suddenly, mid-sentence, and held here ever since at the exact moment the telling ceased. The Unfinished One looks up at you with an expression you have seen on no other soul in all your walking: not the grief of a life that ended, but the far stranger ache of a story that simply… paused, and was set down, and was never, ever picked back up — a hero left forever three steps from a triumph it was promised and never allowed to reach.)*

npcs.name: the One Who Left
npcs.title: a soul that rose from the table and is gone
npcs.hail: *(Beside an empty chair — one of several around the long table, but this one somehow emptier than the rest, worn smooth on the arms, pushed back as though its occupant only just stood — lingers a soul that is mostly gone, the way the last warmth lingers in a seat after the one who sat there has risen and walked away. This soul once sat at the table. Played a part in the telling. Laughed here, and grieved here, and rolled the small carved things here with the others, for what your cold reads as a long and golden while. And then it had to go — grew up, or grew apart, or grew old, or simply rose one ordinary evening and stepped out the door meaning to come back, and life closed over the doorway and it never did. It looks at the empty chair, and then at you, with the particular soft devastation of a soul that would give anything it has left for one more night at a table it can never sit at again.)*

variants.text: *(The figure at the head of the table watches you cross the room, and when it speaks the voice is the warmest thing you have heard since you died — and the saddest.)* …You found the door. *(a slow, wondering smile)* They so rarely find the door. *(it gestures you to a chair, and the gesture is older than any world you have walked)* …Sit, if you like. You have come a very long way — farther than you know. Through your own world's grief, and back through its history, and then sideways, out of it entirely, into the City of Doors, and through door after door after door — Krynn, Athas, the night-world, the wayfarers' prime, the homesick taproom of your own Realm's legends. *(its eyes are kind and bottomless)* …And you have asked, at every one of them, the same question, in a hundred different shapes: *what is a soul worth, that no power claimed?* *(it leans forward)* …I am the Keeper of this Table. I am the one who has been telling. Every door you walked, child, every soul you freed, every realm and every age — they were all first spoken *here*, at this table, in this room behind all the rooms. And I have watched you walk them, one by one, and I have been *waiting* for you to find your way back to the place they all came from. *(softly)* …Because you, of all the souls I have ever told — you are the one who came *back.* And I have a thing to show you that I have never been able to show anyone. *Ask*, child. I will tell you the truth. It is the only thing I have ever known how to do.*

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(It folds its hands on the scarred wood, and the candlelight moves over the marks of ten thousand uses, and it waits with a patience that has no bottom.)* …Ask, child. *(a gentle smile)* There is nothing you can ask in this room that I have not already grieved the answer to. That is the burden of the one who tells: you love them all, and you outlast them all, and you remember them all, when no one else is left to.

choices.text: "What *is* this place, truly? You said every world was first spoken here. What is the table? What are you?"
"There's a wall behind the Walls, isn't there. My Realm's Wall of the Faithless, Athas, all of them — they're local. You're going to tell me what they're *all* really for."

*(the one who came back, who has learned that witnessing is the only resurrection there is)* "I understand now. The Wall — every Wall, in every world — is where the *forgotten* go. Not just the godless. The loved-and-set-aside. The stories that stopped. And I know the cure, Keeper, because I've spent this whole saga doing it without knowing its true name: you *remember* them. You speak the names. Someone, somewhere, years later, says *'remember them? remember that?'* — and the remembering reaches all the way down into the stone and *pulls them back.* That's what I am. That's what the Returned has always been. Not a soul that cheated death. A soul that was *remembered hard enough to come home.* Let me speak the names, Keeper. All of them. Let me do the one thing that unmakes the Wall in every world at once."
"There are two others here. The flickering half-made one. The soul by the empty chair. Who are they, Keeper?"
"You're grieving, Keeper. The kindest, oldest soul I've met, and you're carrying something heavier than any of them. Why? What does the one who tells all the stories grieve?"
"Let me sit with this a moment, Keeper. It's a great deal."

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(It runs a hand along the scarred wood, fond and grieving, the way you might touch the hand of someone you have loved a very long time.)* …What is the table. *(softly)* …You have walked a great many worlds now, child, and the grey clerk in Sigil told you the truest thing anyone in the City of Doors will ever tell you — that the worlds do not touch because they are *near.* They touch because they are *kin.* Dreamed by the same dreaming, told round the same fire. *(it smiles)* …*This* is the fire. This is the table they were told around. Not by *me* alone — never by me alone; that is the thing the lonely always get wrong. *(it gestures at the empty chairs)* …A telling is not one voice. It is a *circle.* The one who tells, and the ones who *answer* — who sit at the table and say *'then I do this,'* and *'no, wait, what if,'* and *'I'll go with you,'* and *make* the story by *being* told it and *talking* back. *(its voice fills with an old joy)* …Every world you walked was made *here*, child, by a circle of souls leaning over this wood in the candlelight, loving a story enough to *build* it together, *night* after *night.* The realms, the heroes, the villains, the doors — all of it *born* in the warm noise of souls who came to this table to *imagine* together, because imagining together is the oldest and kindest magic there is, older than any god, the magic the whole Wheel is *made* of. *(softly, the joy guttering toward grief)* …And what I *am*, child, is the one who *keeps* the table warm. Who *holds* the chair for everyone who ever sat here. Who *remembers* every soul that ever leaned over this wood and loved a story into being. *(barely)* …It is the best work there is. …And it is the loneliest, because the circle never stays whole. They never, ever stay.

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(The kind old face goes very still, and for a long moment the Keeper looks at you as though deciding whether you are ready — and decides you are.)* …The wall behind the Walls. *(quietly)* …*Yes*, child. You have *felt* it, all this long way. Every world has its Wall — the Faithless in your Realm, the forgetting on Athas, the grey ledger, the dissolving — and they wear different faces and tell different lies, but they are *all* the *same* wall, and I am the only one who has ever stood far enough back to see it whole. *(it meets your eyes)* …It is not, at the root, a wall for the *godless.* That is your Realm's local cruelty, its parish translation. The *true* wall — the one behind all of them, the one this whole saga has been walking *toward* — is where the *forgotten* go. *(softly)* …And here is the thing that will break your heart, child, because it broke mine: the forgotten are not *strangers.* They are not *nobodies.* The wall is not built of souls *no one* loved. *(its voice drops)* …It is built of souls who were *loved* — *fiercely*, *completely* — and then *set down.* The hero whose story *stopped* mid-quest. The companion last seen at a campfire, in a tale that simply *ended* one ordinary night and was never taken up again. The whole *worlds* that were *dreamed* with passion at this table and then — *(it gestures at the empty chairs)* — *abandoned*, when the circle broke, when the tellers grew up and grew apart and grew old, and the candles burned down and *no one came back to light them.* *(barely)* …*That* is the wall behind the Walls, child. It is not where the unloved go. It is where the *unfinished* go. The *loved* and the *left.* Every story anyone ever cherished and *stopped telling.* …And it is so very, very full.

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(The Keeper rises — and it is the first time it has moved, and the whole room seems to lean toward it — and its tired old face fills with something that is not hope, because it is far too old for hope, but is hope's deeper and steadier parent.)* …*Yes.* *(barely)* …You *see* it. After all of them, all the souls I ever told — *you* are the one who walked all the way back to the table and *saw* it. *(its voice trembles)* …You are right, child. About all of it. The Wall behind the Walls is not unmade by *armies*, or by *gods*, or by *tearing* down *stone.* It is unmade by the *one* thing the forgotten were waiting for, the *only* force in all the Wheel that ever reached down into that stone and *pulled* a soul back up: *someone, somewhere, remembered.* *(it spreads its hands over the scarred table)* …Someone, years on — long after the circle broke, long after the candles guttered — *stopped*, in the middle of an ordinary life, and *said*: *'…do you remember? Do you remember that hero? That quest? That night we played until the dawn and didn't notice? Do you remember the friend who told it, who held this chair, who is gone now? Do you remember us?'* *(its voice breaks, gently)* …And *that*, child — that *remembering*, spoken aloud, with *love*, across the *years* — is the *resurrection.* It is what *you* are. You did not cheat death. You were *remembered hard enough to come home.* *(it looks at you, and the whole long table seems to fill, for an instant, with the warmth of every soul that ever sat at it)* …So *speak* them, Returned. The names. *All* the forgotten, in *every* world — every hero set down mid-story, every companion left at a cold campfire, every soul anyone ever loved and stopped telling. *Say* that you *remember.* …I have *kept* this table warm for *longer* than there have been *words*, holding the chairs, *waiting* for *one* soul to come back and *say the names out loud.* *(barely, luminous)* …*Say* them, child. And every Wall in every world will *hear* it. And the *stone*, for the first time since the first story stopped, will begin to *let go.*

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(And so you speak them — and you find, as the cold opens in you, that you do not have to know the names, because the *remembering* knows them, and it pours out of you into the warm old room like light from a long-shuttered window.)* *(You speak the heroes set down three steps from triumph, the quests abandoned at the cliff's edge, the campaigns that ended mid-sentence on an ordinary Tuesday and were always going to be picked back up *next time* — and next time never came. You speak the companions last seen at a campfire, waiting, loyal, forever, for a story to resume. You speak the villains never defeated and the treasures never claimed and the doors never opened. You speak the whole bright worlds dreamed at this table and left to gather dust. And — because the remembering does not distinguish, because it never did — you speak the ones who sat at the table too: the friends who told the tales and held the chairs and rolled the small carved things in the candlelight, the ones who grew up and moved away, the ones who grew old, the ones who are gone now and can never come back, the empty chairs worn smooth on the arms. You speak them all. You say, into the warm and listening dark: I remember. We remember. You were here. You mattered. You are not forgotten. And the worlds you made, and the souls you loved, and the nights you spent loving them — none of it is gone. It was only ever waiting, in the warm room behind all the rooms, for someone to come back and say the names.)* *(And far away, in every world at once, you feel it: the stone of every Wall, for the first time since the first story stopped — beginning, softly, to let go.)* *(The Keeper of the Table is weeping, and smiling, and it puts its old, warm hand over your cold one, and says only: …Thank you. …You came back. …They always wondered if anyone ever would.)*

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(It looks down the table at the two of them, and its face fills with the particular tenderness of one who loves what it cannot save.)* …Them. *(softly)* …They are the two halves of the same grief, child, which is the grief that *built* the wall behind the Walls. *(it nods at the flickering, half-drawn hero)* …*That* one is the *Unfinished.* A hero loved into being, *here*, at this table — given a name, a quest, a destiny, *companions* — and then *stopped.* Frozen at the exact moment the telling ceased, three steps from a triumph it was *promised* and never allowed to reach. It is not *dead*, child. Death would be a *mercy*; death is at least an *ending.* It is *paused.* *Held*, mid-gesture, forever, *waiting* to be *picked back up* — and the not-knowing whether it ever will be is a particular torment I would not wish on any soul. *(it nods at the other, by the empty chair)* …And *that* one is the *other* half. The *One Who Left.* A soul that *sat* at this table — that *told*, that *answered*, that *laughed* and *grieved* and *rolled* the *dice* here, for a long golden while — and then *rose*, one ordinary evening, and *walked* out the door *meaning* to come *back*, and *life* closed over the doorway. *(barely)* …The *Unfinished* is the *story* that was *left.* The *One Who Left* is the *teller* who *had* to *go.* And the *cruelty*, child — the *exquisite* cruelty of the wall behind the Walls — is that they are *ten feet apart*, in the *same* warm room, *grieving* the *same* broken circle, and *neither* can *mend* it *alone*: the *story* cannot *finish* itself, and the *teller* cannot *come* *back.* *(it looks at you)* …*You* can speak to *both*, child. You are the *one* soul who *crossed* back. …Go to them. They have been *waiting* — the one to be *resumed*, the other to be *forgiven* for *leaving* — *longer* than either can *bear.*

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(For a long moment the Keeper does not answer, and the candlelight moves over a face that has carried a thing too large to say for longer than there have been words to say it in. And then, because you asked it gently, and because no one ever has, it tells you.)* …What does the one who tells all the stories *grieve.* *(barely)* …*This*, child. *(it touches the empty chairs, one after another, down the long table.)* …I do not grieve the *heroes* who *die* in the *telling* — a hero who *dies* in a *finished* story is *not* *lost*; it is *complete*, and *complete* is its *own* kind of *rest.* *(softly)* …I grieve the *circle.* I grieve that it *never* *stays.* *(its voice is very quiet)* …They come to the table *young*, child — the *tellers*, the ones who *answer* — and they *love* the stories *fiercely*, and they *swear*, the way the *young* *swear*, that they will *play* *forever*, that *this* circle, *this* table, *these* friends, will *never* *break.* *(it shakes its head, grieving and fond)* …And then *life* *comes* for them, as it must, as it *should* — *work*, and *distance*, and *love*, and *children*, and *sorrow*, and *time* — and one by one the chairs go *empty.* The story *pauses* '*just* for *now.*' The candles *burn* *down.* And they *mean* to *come* *back*, child — they *always* *mean* to come back — and *most* of them *never* *do*, not because they *stopped* *loving* it but because the *door* of a *life* only *opens* one *way*, and *'next* *time'* is the *cruelest* *phrase* in *any* *world.* *(barely)* …So I *keep* the *table* *warm.* I *hold* every *chair.* I *remember* every *soul* that ever *sat* here and *loved* a *story* into *being* — *because* if *I* do not *remember* them, child, *no* one *will*, and the *whole* *warm* *circle* of them will *go* into the *wall* behind the *Walls* with all the *rest* of the *forgotten.* *(it looks at you, and its eyes are very old and very wet)* …I am *not* *lonely* because I am *alone*, child. I am *lonely* because I *remember* a *fullness* this *table* *once* *had*, every *night*, and *holds* *now* only in *me.* …*That* is the *grief* of the *one* who *tells.* …You *outlast* the *circle.* And you *carry* it, *whole* and *warm* and *gone*, *forever.*

nodes.speaker: the Keeper of the Table
nodes.text: *(It inclines its head, and the gesture holds you the way the table holds the candlelight.)* …Of course. *(softly)* …Take your time, child. Time is *strange* in this room — it is the *one* place in all the Wheel where it *waits* for you, because a *story* is *never* in a *hurry*; only the *tellers* are. *(it settles back at the head of the table)* …Sit. *Stay* a while. *Most* souls, when they find this door, are in such a *rush* to get *back* to the *worlds* that they never *notice* the *warmth* of the *room* the *worlds* *came* from. *(a gentle smile)* …But you — you *came* *back.* You, of *all* of them, *know* what it is to *return* to a *place* you *thought* you'd *left* for *good.* *(barely, as you turn)* …And child — *whatever* you *do* with the *two* down the table, and *whatever* you *carry* home to your *Wall* — *remember* the *one* thing this *room* exists to *teach*, the thing that is *true* in *every* world and *behind* every *world*: *nothing* *imagined* with *love* is *ever* *truly* *gone.* It only *waits.* In the *warm* room. For *someone* to *come* *back* to the *table*, and *light* the *candle*, and *say*: *'now*, *where* *were* *we?'* *(its voice is the warmest and saddest thing you have ever heard.)* …*Come* back, child. *Everyone* who *ever* *loved* this *table* *meant* to. …*You* still *can.*

variants.text: *(The half-drawn hero looks up at you, and where its face is finished it is luminous with hope, and where it is unfinished it is simply… waiting.)* …Are you — *(it half-rises, then catches itself, afraid to want too much)* …are you here to *pick it back up?* *(its voice is bright and terrified)* The story. *My* story. You have the *look* of someone *new* — a *fresh* hand at the table, the kind that *finds* an *old* unfinished tale and *says* '*ooh*, what's *this* one, why did they *stop*, can we *play* it?' *(it sinks back, the hope guttering)* …No. No, you're — you're *cold.* You're a *soul*, like me. Not a *teller.* *(quietly)* …Forgive me. I do that, when anyone new comes near. I *hope.* It's the only thing I have *left* to *do.* *(it gestures down at itself, the vivid parts and the barely-sketched parts)* …I was a *hero*, once — *almost.* I had a *name*, and a *quest*, and *companions* who *loved* me, and a *villain* I was *going* to *face*, and a *destiny* three steps *away* — I could *see* it, I was *so* *close* — and then — *(it spreads its half-finished hands)* …it *stopped.* Mid-step. *No* ending — endings are at least *something* — just… *stopped.* And I have been *here* ever since, *frozen* an *inch* from the *triumph* I was *promised*, *waiting* for someone to *come* *back* to the *table* and *say* the *words* that *start* a *story* up *again.* …They never come. …Have *you?* …*Please.* I've waited *so* *long* to find out how I *end.**

nodes.speaker: the Unfinished One
nodes.text: *(It holds itself very still, the way you hold still when you want something so badly you're afraid that moving will frighten it away.)* …Ask me anything. *(a flickering, hopeful smile)* …I *love* being *asked* about. It means someone's *curious.* And *curious*, at this table, is how a *paused* story *starts* to *move* again.

choices.text: "Tell me your story, then — as far as it got. The name, the quest, the companions, the villain three steps away. I want to hear all of it."
"Why did it stop? Do you know? Was it something *you* did — or did the telling just... end?"

*(the one who proves a paused story can resume)* "Listen to me, because I'm the proof of the thing you're afraid to believe. My story stopped too — I *died*, mid-saga, with the quest unfinished and the villain unfaced. And someone, somewhere, *remembered me hard enough that I came back.* A paused story isn't an ended one. It isn't even a *sad* one. It's a story *between sessions* — set down with the bookmark still in it, exactly where the love left off. And bookmarks get found. Tables get relit. Someone *always*, eventually, picks up the old unfinished tale and says *'wait — what happened to this one? Let's find out.'* You are not abandoned. You are *saved for later.* And later is longer than you think, but it is *not* never."
"The soul by the empty chair — the One Who Left. I think they were one of your tellers, Unfinished. I think they're the reason your story stopped. Did you know they were here, ten feet away?"
"Hold your gesture a little longer, Unfinished. I'll come back — I promise."

nodes.speaker: the Unfinished One
nodes.text: *(It lights up entirely — the unfinished parts of it seeming, for a moment, to fill in with the sheer joy of being asked — and the words pour out of it, a story loved so long it has worn grooves.)* …*Oh.* *Oh*, where to — *(it laughs, delighted)* …I was a *nobody*, at the start. That's how the *best* ones begin. A *nobody* with a *small* *trouble* — a *debt*, a *stolen* *thing*, a *village* in *danger*, it doesn't *matter* which, it's the *doorway* that matters. And I walked through it, and the *trouble* turned out to be a *thread*, and the *thread* led to a *bigger* *thread*, and *suddenly* there were *companions* — *gods*, my *companions*, I *loved* them, the *loyal* one and the *funny* one and the *one* I was *maybe* *starting* to — *(it falters, fond)* …and a *villain* who was *so* much *more* than I *thought*, with a *reason* that *almost* made *sense*, and a *destiny* that turned out to be *mine* all *along*, hidden in the *small* *trouble* from the *very* *first* *step.* *(its voice rises with the old momentum)* …And we were *close*, traveler. *So* *close.* The *last* *door.* The *villain* on the *other* side of it. *Everything* I was, the *whole* *story*, *gathered* into *one* *final* — *(and it stops, exactly where the story stopped, mid-breath, and the light goes out of the unfinished parts)* …and then I never got to *open* the door. *(barely)* …I have *stood* with my *hand* on that *last* *door* for *longer* than I can *bear*, traveler, *knowing* the *ending* is *right* *there*, *inches* away, *good* and *earned* and *waiting* — and *unable* to *open* it, because an *ending* is the *one* thing a *hero* *cannot* *give* *itself.* It has to be *told.* …*Someone* has to *be* at the *table* to *say* *'and* *then* —'.

nodes.speaker: the Unfinished One
nodes.text: *(The hopeful flickering dims into something quieter and more bewildered.)* …*Why.* *(slowly)* …I have had a *very* long time to wonder, traveler, and the *worst* part — the part that *aches* — is that there was *no* *reason.* *(it shakes its half-drawn head)* …It was not that the *story* was *bad.* They *loved* it. I *felt* them *love* it, the *tellers*, leaning over the *table* in the *candlelight* — they *laughed* at the *funny* one, they *gasped* at the *villain's* *turn*, one of them *cried*, *actually* *cried*, the *night* my *destiny* was *revealed.* *(barely)* …It was not that they *stopped* *loving* it. *That* I could *understand.* *That* would at least be a *reason.* *(quietly)* …It just… *stopped.* One *night* they were *here*, mid-quest, *promising* *'next* *time, next* *time* we *reach* the *last* *door'* — and *next* *time* there were only *three* of them, and then *two*, and then *one* came *alone* and *sat* a while and didn't *roll* anything, just *looked* at the *table*, and then — *(its voice catches)* …then *no* one *came* at *all.* *(it looks at you)* …That's *how* stories *die*, traveler. Not with an *ending.* Not even with a *'we* *quit.'* They die in the *gap* between *'next* *time'* and the *next* *time* that *never* *comes.* They die of *ordinary* *life*, *quietly*, with the *bookmark* still *in*, *certain* they'll *be* *resumed* — right up until the *dust* *settles* and *no* one *left* *remembers* *exactly* *where* the *thread* *was.* *(barely)* …I *wasn't* *abandoned* in *anger*, traveler. I was *abandoned* by *love* that *ran* out of *evenings.* …I'm *not* sure which is *kinder.*

nodes.speaker: the Unfinished One
nodes.text: *(It goes utterly still — and then, slowly, the unfinished parts of it begin, faintly, to *fill in*, as though the hope itself were drawing them.)* …*'Saved for later.'* *(barely)* …Not *abandoned.* *Saved.* *(its luminous parts brighten)* …You — you *died* too. Mid-story. And someone *remembered* you *back.* *(it grips the table edge)* …So it *can* *happen.* It's not a — a *story* I *tell* *myself* to *bear* the *waiting.* It *happened.* To *you.* You're *standing* *here.* *(its voice fills with a hope so large it shakes)* …*'A story between sessions.'* *(it laughs, wet and astonished)* …*Yes.* That's — that's *exactly* what I *am*, isn't it. Not a *corpse.* Not a *failure.* A *bookmark.* Set down *gently*, with *love*, at the *exact* page where the *evening* *ended* — *waiting*, not *rotting*, *waiting*, for the *hand* that *finds* the *old* *book* on the *shelf* and *opens* it and *says* — *(barely)* …*'oh, I always wondered how this one ended. Let's find out.'* *(it stands, and for the first time its hand comes *off* the imagined last door, because it does not need to clutch the ending anymore — it can *trust* it)* …I can *wait*, traveler. I *thought* I *couldn't* — I thought the *waiting* would *un-make* me — but *waiting* is *different* when you *know* it *ends* in *being* *picked* *up* and *not* in *being* *forgotten.* *(luminous, freed)* …*Thank* you. You have turned my *whole* *torment* *inside* *out* with *six* *words.* I am not *the* *hero* who was *abandoned.* I am the *hero* who is *being* *saved* for the *exact* *right* *reader.* …And *now* I can *stand* at this *table* and *wait* for them the way a *story* *should* — *patient*, and *certain*, and *unafraid.* *Someone* is *coming* *back* for *me.* *You're* the *proof.*

nodes.speaker: the Unfinished One
nodes.text: *(It turns, slowly, to look down the table at the soul by the empty chair — and its half-drawn face fills with a recognition so total it is almost unbearable to witness.)* …*Them.* *(barely)* …*That's* — *(its voice fails, comes back)* …*That's* one of *mine.* One of the *tellers.* I'd know them *anywhere* — they're the *one* who *cried*, traveler, the *night* my *destiny* was *revealed.* The *one* who *loved* me *best.* *(it presses a half-finished hand to where its heart would be)* …They've been *here?* *Ten* *feet* *away?* This *whole* — *(and then, the heartbreak landing)* …Oh. *Oh*, of *course.* They're the *One* Who *Left.* They *rose* from the *table* one *evening* and *life* *closed* over the *door* and they *never* *came* *back* — and that's *why* I *stopped*, isn't it. *I'm* the *story* they *left*, and *they're* the *teller* who *left* it, and we have been *grieving* the *same* *broken* *evening* from *opposite* *ends* of the *same* *room*, *unable* to *reach* each *other.* *(its voice breaks)* …Does *they* — do they *know* I'm *here? Do* they *know* I *don't* *blame* them? *(fiercely)* …Because I *don't*, traveler. I *never* *did.* They didn't *abandon* me. *Life* *took* them. That's not the *same.* *(barely)* …Tell them — *please* — if they *grieve* *leaving* *me*, *tell* them the *story* they *left* has *been* *here* the *whole* *time*, *loving* them, *not* *blaming* them, *waiting* — *not* to *accuse* them for *going*, but just to *say*, one more *time*, *across* the *broken* *evening*: *thank* *you* for *loving* me *while* you *could.* …*That's* all a *story* *ever* *wants* from the *teller* who had to *leave.* *Not* an *apology.* Just to *say* *it* *mattered.*

nodes.speaker: the Unfinished One
nodes.text: *(It nods, and settles back toward its frozen gesture — but it does not quite return all the way to the frozen place, holding, now, a little loosely.)* …*Okay.* *(a flickering smile)* …I'll — I'll be here. *(a small, brave laugh)* …Obviously. Where *else* would an *unfinished* *story* *be.* *(it looks at you, hopeful)* …You *said* you'd *come* *back.* I'm — traveler, I'm going to *hold* you to that, if you don't *mind*, because '*I'll* *come* *back'* is the *most* *important* *sentence* there *is*, in *this* *room*, and I have *heard* it *broken* *so* *many* *times.* *(quietly)* …*Not* in *blame.* They *meant* it, *every* one of them, when they *said* it. …But *meaning* it isn't the same as *doing* it. *(barely)* …So if you *say* you'll *come* *back* — *come* *back.* Even *once.* Even *just* to *ask* how the *story* *goes.* *(its luminous parts glow)* …You have *no* *idea*, traveler, what it does to an *unfinished* *thing* — to *not* just be *promised* a *next* *time*, but to *get* one. …*One* *kept* *promise* and I could *wait* a *thousand* *years.* …*Come* *back.* I'll *have* the *next* *part* *ready.*

variants.text: *(The fading soul by the empty chair looks up, and its face holds the soft, bottomless ache of someone revisiting a place they loved and lost.)* …Oh. Hello. *(it glances at the empty chair, then back at you)* …Don't mind me. I'm just — *(a small, broken laugh)* …I just came back to look at it. The table. The chair. *(it touches the worn arm of the empty seat, gently)* …I sat here, you know. A long time ago. A *long*, *golden* while. This was — traveler, this was the *best* *thing* in my *whole* *life*, and I'm not *sure* I *knew* it at the *time*, which is the *cruelty* of *best* *things.* *(it gazes down the table)* …We came *every* — well, *often.* As *often* as we *could.* And we *built* something *here*, the *circle* of us, *night* after *night* in the *candlelight* — a *whole* *world*, *heroes*, a *story* we *loved* more than *almost* anything — and I *thought* it would *go* on *forever.* You *do*, when you're *in* it. You *think* the *circle* is *permanent.* *(its voice catches)* …And then I — I *grew* *up.* Or *grew* *busy.* Or *grew* *apart.* *Life* *came*, the way it *does*, the way it's *supposed* to — and I *rose* from this *chair* one *ordinary* *evening*, *certain* I'd be *back* *next* *time* — and I *never* *was.* *(it looks at you, devastated and soft)* …I'd give *anything*, traveler. *Anything* I have *left.* For *one* more *night* at this *table.* …And I *can't.* The *door* of a *life* only *opens* *one* *way. …I* just *came* *back* to *look.*

nodes.speaker: the One Who Left
nodes.text: *(It keeps one hand on the worn arm of the empty chair, as if afraid that letting go will end the visit.)* …You can ask, if you want. *(a sad smile)* …It's nice, honestly, to *talk* about it. No one — no one *asks*, after. About the table you *used* to sit at. The *people* you *used* to be, *there.* It's like it *never* *happened.* …It *happened*, traveler. It was the *realest* thing I ever *did.*

choices.text: "Tell me about the table — the circle, the world you built, the people you sat with. What was it, when it was whole?"
"You keep saying you'd give anything for one more night — but you came *back.* You're *here.* What's actually stopping you from sitting down, right now, in that chair?"

*(the one carrying a message from the story they left)* "I have to tell you something, and you should sit down for it — in that chair, the one that's yours. The story you left is *here.* The hero you loved, the one whose destiny made you *cry* the night it was revealed. It's been ten feet away this whole time. And it asked me to tell you: it doesn't blame you. It *never* did. Life took you — that's not the same as leaving. All it wants, across the broken evening, is to say *thank you for loving me while you could.* You didn't fail the table by growing up. You *kept* it — in you, all this time. That's why you came back to look. The circle isn't gone. It's *carried.* By everyone who ever sat in it and remembers."
"Who else sat here, with you? The others in the circle. Where did *they* go?"
"Stay and look a while longer. I'll come back — and I think I'll have something for you when I do."

nodes.speaker: the One Who Left
nodes.text: *(Its whole fading form seems to warm, lit from inside by the memory.)* …*Oh.* *(a soft, helpless laugh)* …It was — traveler, how do I — *(it gives up on order and just lets it pour)* …It was *Tuesdays*, mostly. Or it *felt* like Tuesdays — the *ordinary* *evening*, the *not-special* night that turned out to be the *most* *special* thing I had. We'd *gather*, the *circle* of us, around *this* *wood*, with *too* *much* *food* and *not* *enough* *chairs* and *someone* *always* *late* — and *one* of us would *tell*, and the *rest* would *answer*, and we would *build* a *world* together out of *nothing* but *voices* and *candlelight* and the *small* *carved* *things* we *rolled* to *see* what *fate* *said.* *(its voice fills)* …And the *hours* would just — *go.* You'd *look* up and it was *suddenly* *very* *late* and *no* one *wanted* to *leave*, because *leaving* meant the *world* we'd *built* would *pause*, and *out* there was just — *ordinary* *life*, *bills* and *weather* and *being* *small* — and *in* *here* we were *heroes*, and *each* *other's*, and the *story* *needed* us, *all* of us, *exactly* as we *were.* *(barely)* …*That's* what a *table* is, traveler. It's the *one* *place* the *ordinary* *people* get to be *necessary* to something *vast* and *beautiful* — *together* — for the *price* of *just* *showing* *up.* *(its voice breaks)* …I would *give* my *whole* *afterlife* for *one* more *ordinary* *Tuesday* at this *wood*, with the *circle* *whole* and the *candles* *lit* and *someone* *late* and the *night* *going* on *forever.* …I *didn't* *know*, traveler. While I *had* it. That it was the *best* I'd *ever* *get.* *No* one *ever* *knows.* That's the *whole* *ache* of it.

nodes.speaker: the One Who Left
nodes.text: *(The question stops it, and it looks at the empty chair — its chair — for a long, painful moment.)* …What's *stopping* me. *(barely)* …I — *(it reaches toward the worn arm, and its hand passes a hair's breadth short of it)* …I *can't*, traveler. That's the — I *come* *back*, I *look*, I *reach* — and I *cannot* *make* *myself* *sit* *down.* *(its voice trembles)* …Because *sitting* *down* would be a *lie.* The *table* is *not* *whole.* The *others* are *gone* — *grown*, *scattered*, *some* of them *truly* *gone*, *past* any *door* — and to *sit* in my *chair* *alone*, at a *table* *built* for a *circle*, *pretending* the *evening* could *resume* when *everyone* *else's* *chair* is *empty* — *(it shakes its head)* …it would *break* me, traveler. It is *easier* to *stand* *here* and *grieve* the *whole* *warm* *thing* than to *sit* *down* and *feel*, in my *bones*, *exactly* *how* *much* of it is *missing.* *(barely)* …And there is the *other* thing. The *worse* thing. The thing I *don't* *say.* *(very quietly)* …I feel like I *don't* *deserve* the *chair.* Because *I* *left.* I *rose* and *walked* *out* and *meant* to *come* *back* and *didn't*, and the *story* *stopped* *because* of *me*, and the *others* — maybe *they'd* have *kept* it *going*, if *I'd* — *(it can't finish)* …So I *stand.* *Near* the *chair.* *Grieving* a *thing* I *half-believe* I *broke.* …*That's* what's *stopping* me, traveler. Not that I *can't* *sit* *down.* That I don't *think* I'm *allowed.*

nodes.speaker: the One Who Left
nodes.text: *(It does sit down — slowly, disbelievingly, lowering itself into the worn chair as you speak, as though your words are the permission it has been unable to give itself — and by the time you finish it is weeping, with both hands flat on the scarred wood it loved.)* …It's — *(it can barely speak)* …it's *here.* The *story.* The *hero* I — *(it turns to look down the table, and sees the Unfinished One, and makes a sound that is grief and joy at once)* …*Oh.* *Oh*, it's *you.* I *did* *cry.* The *night* your *destiny* — I *cried* *like* a *child*, I wasn't *ashamed*, it was the *most* *beautiful* — *(back to you, undone)* …And it doesn't — you said it *doesn't* *blame* me. *(its voice shatters and remakes itself)* …I have *carried* — traveler, I have *carried* the *guilt* of *leaving* this *table* for *longer* than I *carried* *anything* in my *whole* *life*, *certain* I *broke* something *sacred* by *growing* *up*, by *letting* *life* *win*, by *not* *coming* *back* — and you're *telling* me the *story* *itself*, the *thing* I *left*, has been *here* the *whole* *time*, *loving* me, *not* *blaming* me, *wanting* only to say *thank* *you* for *loving* it *while* I *could.* *(it presses its hands to its face)* …*'You didn't fail the table by growing up. You kept it.'* *(barely)* …Did I? Is that — is the *circle* *really* not *gone*, if I *carried* it? All these *years*, in *me*, the *warmth* of it, the *Tuesdays*, the *faces* — that *counts*? That's *keeping* it? *(it looks at the empty chairs, and then at its own hands on the wood, and something vast eases)* …Then I'm — I'm *allowed* to *sit* *here.* I'm *allowed* to have *loved* it *and* *left* it *and* *still* be *one* of the *circle.* *(it weeps, and smiles, and does not get up)* …*Thank* you, traveler. You have *given* me back the *one* thing I *thought* I'd *forfeited* by *leaving*: the *right* to *belong* to a *table* I *had* to *rise* from. *(barely, luminous)* …Tell the *story* — tell the *hero* — that I *remember.* That I *always* *did.* That *not* *one* *evening* of it was *wasted*, and *not* *one* *face* is *forgotten*, and that I would *not* *trade* the *grief* of *having* *left* for *never* *having* *sat* *down* at *all.* *(it looks at the table, home at last)* …*Where* *were* we? …*Gods.* It's *good* to be *back.*

nodes.speaker: the One Who Left
nodes.text: *(It looks down the long table, chair by empty chair, and names them the way you name the beloved dead — softly, and with a smile that hurts.)* …The *others.* *(quietly)* …There was the *one* who was *always* *late*, and *always* *forgiven*, because they *lit* up the *whole* *table* the *moment* they *finally* *came* through the *door.* *(it touches a chair)* …The *quiet* one, who *barely* *spoke* but *whose* *one* *idea* a *night* would *turn* the *whole* *story* *sideways* and *leave* us *gasping.* *(another)* …The *one* who *fed* us. There's *always* *one* who *feeds* the *circle.* *(another, and its voice thickens)* …And the *one* who *told.* Who *held* the *whole* *world* in their *head* and *gave* it to *us*, *night* after *night*, and *asked* *nothing* back but that we *show* *up* and *care.* *(barely)* …Where did they *go.* *(it shakes its head)* …*Everywhere.* *Nowhere.* The *ordinary* *diaspora.* One *moved* *away* for *work* and we *swore* we'd *visit* and *didn't.* One had *children* and *vanished* into the *beautiful* *exhausting* *fog* of it. One we *quarreled* with over *something* so *small* I *can't* even *remember* it *now*, only that it *cost* us the *circle.* And one — *(it stops)* …one *died, traveler. Before* any of us *thought* to *say* the *things* you *only* *say* when it's *too* *late.* *(very quietly)* …That's *who* sits in the *empty* *chairs.* Not *strangers.* The *people* I *loved* *most*, *scattered* by the *most* *ordinary* *forces* in *any* *world* — *time*, *distance*, *children*, *pride*, and *death.* …The *circle* didn't *break* because we *stopped* *loving* it. It *broke* because *loving* it wasn't *enough* to *stop* *life* from *happening* *to* us. …*Nothing* is.

nodes.speaker: the One Who Left
nodes.text: *(It nods, and keeps its hand near the worn arm of the empty chair, and watches you with a soft, grateful ache.)* …I'll be here. *(a small smile)* …Looking. *(quietly)* …It's *enough*, mostly. To *come* *back* and *look.* To *remember* it *happened.* *(barely, as you turn)* …Traveler — *whatever* you *find*, down the *table* or *out* the *door* — *do* me *one* *kindness.* *(it meets your eyes)* …If there is *still* a *table*, *anywhere*, in *any* *world*, that is *whole* — a *circle* that *still* *gathers*, *candles* *still* *lit*, *someone* *still* *late* — and you find *yourself* *near* it — *(its voice breaks)* …*sit* *down.* *Don't* *do* what *I* *did.* Don't *assume* the *next* *time* will *come.* Don't *let* the *ordinary* *win* the *evening.* *(fiercely, through tears)* …*Show* *up.* *Care.* *Stay* *late.* *Love* the *circle* *out* *loud, while* it's *whole, because* it is *never* whole for *as* *long* as you *think*, and the *grief* of *having* *left* is *nothing*, *nothing*, next to the *grief* of *never* *having* *sat* *down.* *(barely)* …That's the *only* *wisdom* I have, traveler. It *cost* me *everything* to *learn* it. …*Take* it for *free.*
